metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit file paths or document section anchors for every edit target: the spec document containing A1 and T5a, the location of 'verifier blast_radius attribute', and the source of 'impact arithmetic'.
findings.finding: The target locations for changes are not specified: 'A1', 'T5a', and 'verifier attribute' are referenced without identifying the file path, section heading, or document where an executor would make the edits.
findings.location: What section: 'G-9: small-scale default in A1 + T5a' and 'FP-3(b): make the existing blast_radius... explicit and uniform on the verifier blast_radius attribute'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the eval harness for AC3: name the test suite / golden dataset, the run command, and the numeric threshold (e.g. 'zero drop in TP rate on the N labelled high-scale findings in eval/fixtures/') that constitutes passing.
findings.finding: AC3 'Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings' defers the definition of the evaluation harness to the executor — no test suite, dataset, or run command is named, making this a placeholder rather than a decision.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC1 and AC2 (e.g. a grep/schema assertion confirming the small-scale default text and the one-way ratchet phrase are present in the target spec files).
findings.finding: No proving command exists for AC1 ('A1/T5a scale anchors') or AC2 ('Explicit one-way blast_radius phrasing'); the plan describes text additions but provides no concrete check — even a grep or diff — to confirm the additions are present and correct.
findings.suggested_fix: e.g. `grep -c 'blast_radius only lowers' path/to/verifier_spec.md` asserts the phrase is present; similar for the scale anchor text in A1/T5a.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a described work item for the AC3 regression eval: specify what corpus/cases constitute 'genuine high-scale/high-blast findings', how the eval is run, and the pass/fail threshold.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings') has no corresponding described work in the plan body — there is no described eval methodology, dataset, or process for conducting or validating this regression check, making it an orphan criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a bullet such as: 'Regression eval: run the updated G-9/FP-3(b) rubric against a reference set of ≥N known high-scale or high-blast findings (drawn from [source]) and confirm all are still flagged at the expected severity; document pass/fail threshold.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the rubric changes (e.g., version the rubric file so the prior A1/T5a/FP-3(b) text is recoverable, or specify a feature flag to disable the new anchors during a validation window).
findings.finding: The plan modifies existing behavior of criteria A1, T5a, and FP-3(b) without describing a rollback path or expand-contract sequence if the new calibration anchors produce worse outcomes than the current behavior.
findings.location: What section — G-9 and FP-3(b) bullets

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file/section containing the verifier `blast_radius` attribute and the impact arithmetic so the one-way ratchet edit location is unambiguous and the confirmation step is verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan specifies making the blast_radius one-way ratchet 'explicit and uniform on the verifier attribute' but does not name the file, section, or artifact where this verifier attribute lives, leaving the concrete edit location ambiguous and making future updates harder to discover.
findings.location: FP-3(b) section / 'explicit one-way blast_radius phrasing on the verifier attribute' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'Edit: `rubric/verifier_schema.md` §blast_radius (or equivalent), cross-check against the impact formula in `rubric/scoring.md` §impact-arithmetic.' — matching the level of specificity used for G-9 (which names A1 and T5a explicitly).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a complementary eval criterion (or golden-set test cases) that verifies the new scale anchors and prohibited-reasoning rules actually SUPPRESS inflated findings: e.g., 'reviewer presented with a small-scale change and no stated estimate/profiling/AC produces no high-scale finding' and 'blast_radius attribute never increases relative to its input value across the test suite'.
findings.finding: The eval AC (AC3) only tests one direction of the new behavior — that genuine high-scale/high-blast findings still fire. There is no eval criterion testing that the new G-9 scale anchors and prohibited-reasoning rules actually SUPPRESS inflated or manufactured findings. The primary motivation of WS6 is preventing false positives, yet the acceptance criteria contain no test that a reviewer applying the new rules fails to inflate a small-scale change into a high-severity finding.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '[ ] Eval confirms suppression: a representative set of small-scale change plans (no stated estimate, no profiling, no scale-related AC) produces zero high-scale or high-blast findings after WS6; AND the blast_radius value for any finding never exceeds its pre-ratchet value in the test suite.' Pair with AC3 to make the eval two-directional.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the file/section (e.g. a criteria prompt file, a new section in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md`, or a new `.md` in `docs/calibration/`) where G-9 will live, so its existence and content are verifiable against the codebase.
findings.finding: G-9 is referenced as an established rule identifier (in 'What' and 'AC1') but returns zero matches anywhere in the codebase, docs, or criteria registry. It cannot be looked up, cross-referenced, or verified from plan text alone — it is an entirely new label with no prior existence.
findings.location: ## What — 'G-9: small-scale default in A1 + T5a; prohibited-reasoning examples…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the plan such as: 'G-9 will be authored as a new subsection in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md` (or a new `docs/calibration/G9_scale_anchors.md`) and injected into `plan_review_T5a.md` and the A1 criterion prompt.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'FP-3(b)' with the actual artifact location where the 'blast_radius lowers only' constraint will be added (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md` SEVERITY ATTRIBUTES block and/or `verify.py:VERIFIER_RULES`), or add FP-3(b) to the observed-false-positives.md taxonomy first.
findings.finding: FP-3(b) is referenced as an established label ('make the existing FP-3(b) one-way ratchet EXPLICIT') but returns zero matches anywhere in the codebase. The only existing FP taxonomy is in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md`, which lists FP1–FP7 — no FP-3(b) entry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all three verifier preamble files that need the explicit one-way blast_radius ratchet phrasing: plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, and code-review-verify.md (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/).
findings.finding: The plan says the blast_radius one-way ratchet should be made 'explicit and uniform on the verifier attribute' but does not enumerate all three verifier preamble files that carry blast_radius prose: plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, AND code-review-verify.md. If 'uniform' means the ratchet language is consistent across all gate verifier preambles, code-review-verify.md is an unenumerated consumer that the edit must also reach.
findings.location: What / FP-3(b) — 'explicit one-way blast_radius phrasing on the verifier attribute'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Edit plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, and code-review-verify.md to add the explicit one-way ratchet sentence: blast_radius is a tiebreaker that ONLY LOWERS impact, never raises it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 which eval file(s) to extend (e.g., plan-review-finder.eval.yaml) and what kind of cases confirm non-regression: at minimum one new recall case where a genuinely high-scale/high-blast finding still fires after the G-9 anchors are added.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings') names no specific eval file, dataset, or case-type to extend. The codebase has an existing plan-review finder eval at src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml with the false-fire case FP5-T5a already in it; the plan does not say whether AC3 is satisfied by adding cases there, by a separate verifier-focused eval, or by some other mechanism — leaving the acceptance criterion unactionably vague for an implementer.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define G-9's complete value vocabulary — the exact small-scale default anchor text, the full prohibited-reasoning phrase set (not just one example), and the two-directional bar wording — and name the target files (e.g. plan_review_A1.md, plan_review_T5a.md, or a shared scaffold) where each element will be inserted.
findings.finding: G-9 is referenced as a named, resolvable rule identifier ('G-9: small-scale default in A1 + T5a') in both the What section and AC1, but zero matches for 'G-9' exist anywhere in the codebase, docs, criteria_routing.json, or any reviewer prompt. Its full value vocabulary — the exact anchor text for 'small-scale default', the complete set of 'prohibited-reasoning examples', and the 'two-directional bar' phrasing — is never defined in the plan; only a fragmentary description and one illustrative phrase are provided.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section, AC1: 'A1/T5a scale anchors (small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the exact artifacts where the one-way blast_radius ratchet must appear (at minimum: SeverityAttrs.blast_radius field description in review_kernel/verify.py, plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, and code-review-verify.md) and specify the exact phrasing to add to each so AC2's 'explicit and uniform' bar is mechanically checkable.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 specifies making the 'blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises' one-way ratchet 'EXPLICIT and UNIFORM on the verifier blast_radius attribute,' but the ratchet phrase is absent from the verifier schema and all three verifier preamble prompts, and the plan does not name which artifacts to edit.
findings.location: Plan AC2: 'Explicit one-way blast_radius phrasing on the verifier attribute'; review_kernel/verify.py SeverityAttrs.blast_radius field; plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, code-review-verify.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add to SeverityAttrs.blast_radius Field description: `description='local|module|system — scores the FLAW's reach, not the feature's; blast_radius can only LOWER severity relative to other attributes, never raise it above what prod_impact+likelihood justify'`. Mirror the constraint in the same sentence in each verifier preamble's blast_radius bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify AC3's eval methodology: name the eval harness or test file, describe the sample (e.g. N findings with confirmed blast_radius=system or high scale), and state the pass bar (e.g. 'all genuine high-blast findings still reach advisory or block decision').
findings.finding: AC3 ('Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings') has no corresponding methodology in the plan body: no eval name, no dataset or sample, no pass/fail bar, and no test file location are specified.
findings.location: Plan AC3: 'Eval confirms no regression in genuine high-scale/high-blast findings'
